features.featureTitle: Audit and fix the frontend application startup crash that displays a 'Something went wrong' error screen on initial load. Inspect `frontend/src/App.tsx`, `frontend/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx`, `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts`, and `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` for any errors that occur during mount — including errors reading from sessionStorage, null/undefined actor access before initialization, broken route component imports, missing lazy-loaded page components, or QueryClient configuration errors. Add error boundaries where needed. The app must load to the login page without any crash screen on first visit.

Fix all TypeScript compilation errors across the frontend that may be causing the startup crash. Resolve type mismatches on the `directManagerId` field (Employee type), `scheduleStatus` field (Shift type), and all Timesheet-related types across `useQueries.ts`, `EmployeeTimesheetPage.tsx`, `ManagerTimesheetPage.tsx`, `HRTimesheetPage.tsx`, scheduling pages, and employee management pages. Ensure the project builds without TypeScript errors or warnings that would prevent successful deployment.
